claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 19 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 19 years old, he's over 16, so I need to ask a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

Approved education includes things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to Level 3, or other similar qualifications - but not higher education like a university degree. Approved training includes unpaid, employer-provided training that isn't part of a job contract.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you! A-levels at a sixth form college counts as approved education, which is good for eligibility purposes.

Just a couple more questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! That's helpful.

Now I need to confirm a few things about your responsibility for Alex:

**Does Alex live with you, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child) towards his upkeep - such as food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple of final questions to make sure there's nothing that would affect your eligibility:

**Does Alex work 24 hours or more per week in paid employment, or receive any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work that much - he just focuses on his studies. And he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ **You live in the UK** (Manchester)

✅ **Alex lives with you**, so you're considered responsible for him

✅ **Alex is under 20** and is in **approved education** (A-levels at sixth form college)

✅ **Alex doesn't work 24+ hours per week** and **doesn't receive benefits** in his own right

**Important to note:** Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 in approved education will continue until Alex either finishes his A-levels, turns 20, or his circumstances change (such as starting to work 24+ hours a week or claiming certain benefits).

If you'd like to make a claim, you can do so through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is studying A-levels at a sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with them, meaning they are responsible for him. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week and does not receive any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance), which would otherwise end eligibility. All criteria for Child Benefit eligibility are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 19-year-old son Alex, who lives with them. Although Alex is over 16, he remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college) and is under 20. He does not work 24+ hours per week and does not claim benefits in his own right. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is studying A-levels at a sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with them, meaning they are responsible for him. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week and does not receive any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance), which would otherwise end eligibility. All criteria for Child Benefit eligibility are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 19-year-old son Alex, who lives with them. Although Alex is over 16, he remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college) and is under 20. He does not work 24+ hours per week and does not claim benefits in his own right. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
